definition: As median voter age rises (Japan ~55, Italy ~52, Germany ~49, US ~47), the electorate shifts from net taxpayers to net transfer recipients, systematically producing fiscal expansion. Central bank independence erodes through appointment dynamics, mandate expansion, and implicit coordination. This provides the micro-foundation for financial repression as the path of least resistance.
evidence: Rising median voter ages: Japan ~55, Italy ~52, Germany ~49, US ~47
Positive correlation between population aging and government social spending (Sanz and Velazquez 2007)
Japan template: BoJ-MoF coordination became effectively implicit fiscal financing
CB independence erodes through appointment dynamics, mandate expansion, implicit coordination
Financial repression emerges as path of least resistance, not deliberate policy choice
open_questions: Can institutional design (constitutional fiscal rules) sustainably resist the political economy pressure?
What is the timeline from median voter aging to measurable CB independence erosion?
Does the Japan template of implicit fiscal financing generalize to other institutional contexts?
